notes: Enumerated set of sinks + entry-points from PACKET-VETLOCK-ASSURANCE §3.5 (which cites PACKET-VETLOCK-STARTUP §3.5). FLOOR not CEILING (packet rule): enumeration is the minimum coverage the gate enforces; chokepoints (dataflow, ADR-0011) may catch novel spellings for free. Only `code-execution` is fully enumerated in the packet; other classes are placeholders pending panel enumeration.

sinks.description: The `eval` intrinsic — executes a source string in the caller's scope.
The `Function` constructor — compiles a source string into a callable, effectively `eval` with fresh scope.
Node's `vm` module family (runInThisContext, runInNewContext, runInContext, compileFunction, Script). Executes code in a V8 context that shares the process.
Spawns a shell and runs a command line — the highest-impact sink because it interprets metacharacters.
Directly execve()s a binary with argv — the packet lists it as a sibling of `exec`; missing this while catching `exec` is the classic incomplete-fix.
Spawns a child process; the async lower-level primitive `exec`/`execFile` are layered on.
Spawns a new Node process running a script file with an IPC channel.
Synchronous variant of `exec`; same shell-interpretation surface.
Node's `worker_threads` — spawns a Worker running a JS script (file or eval) in a separate V8 isolate but the same process.
Internal Node primitive that grants access to native bindings from user code. Deprecated but still callable.
Loading a native `.node` addon (via `require('...')` or `process.dlopen`) — arbitrary machine code runs in-process at load.
Compiles and instantiates a WASM module. Arbitrary WASM executes in-process (sandboxed but still Turing-complete).
Compiles a WASM module without instantiating; paired with `new WebAssembly.Instance` for the execution step.
Dynamic `require(<computed>)` — module id is a runtime value, so the resolved target can hide behind string ops. The dataflow chokepoint (ADR-0011) is any value flowing to a require call.
Dynamic ESM `import(<computed>)` — the ESM sibling of `require(<computed>)`. Same chokepoint applies.
The Node `Module` constructor — reachable via `module.constructor` at runtime, allows constructing and compiling arbitrary source into a module in the current process.

net-egress.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: fetch, http/https/http2, net.Socket, dgram, dns.*, WebSocket, EventSource, plus per-library helpers (axios, got, node-fetch).

fs-write.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: fs.write*, fs.appendFile*, fs.createWriteStream, fs.rename, fs.symlink, fs.mkdir, fs.rmdir/rm, fs.chmod, fs.utimes plus their promises/sync variants.

env/secret-read.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: process.env access (whole-object and per-key), dotenv/config libraries, fs reads of ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_*, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, ~/.env variants.

persistence.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: fs writes to shell rc files (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.profile), crontab/at, launchd/systemd unit files, npm/pip pre/postinstall registration, PATH-shadowing, git-hooks writes.

obfuscation/decode.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: atob/Buffer.from(*, 'base64'|'hex'), zlib.inflate/gunzip, rot13/xor helpers, decodeURIComponent chains, unicode-escape decoders, plus the encoded-decode-and-run patterns already defanged in defang/guard.ts.

crypto-mine.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: WebAssembly modules with mining-shaped opcodes, references to known miner pools (defanged), stratum protocol strings, references to CoinHive-family libraries.

clipboard.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: shell-outs to `pbcopy`/`pbpaste`/`xclip`/`xsel`/`clip.exe`, references to `navigator.clipboard.*`, node-clipboard style helpers.

process-enumeration.note: TBD — panel enumeration. Anticipated: `ps`/`tasklist` shell-outs, `/proc` reads on Linux, `os.userInfo`, `os.networkInterfaces`, `os.hostname`, references to running-process lists via native modules.

execution.description: The `preinstall` npm lifecycle script — runs before install. Attacker's highest-yield surface because it fires on `npm install`.
The `install` npm lifecycle script — runs during install, before `postinstall`.
The `postinstall` npm lifecycle script — runs after install. Also runs on transitive dependencies by default in npm ≥7.
The `prepare` npm lifecycle script — runs on `npm install` (no args) for local packages and on `npm pack`/`npm publish`.
The `prepublish` / `prepublishOnly` scripts — attacker-published packages can carry payloads here that fire in maintainer environments.
The `main` field in package.json — resolved when a consumer `require`s the package (top-level module code executes at require time).
The `module` field — ESM equivalent of `main`; resolved for ESM importers, top-level module code runs on import.
The `exports` map in package.json — per-condition subpath map. Every reachable subpath is an entry point for its top-level code.
The `bin` field — installed as an executable in node_modules/.bin. Runs when the user invokes the command from a script or CLI.
Any top-level statement in a package's entry module — runs the first time the module is loaded. Not a distinct npm field, but the mechanism by which every entry point actually gains control.
Side-effects triggered by an `import` statement in the package's ESM entry — the ESM sibling of `top-level-side-effects`.
Test framework hooks (jest.setupFilesAfterEach, mocha `--require`, vitest globalSetup) — executed by CI pipelines that install the package.
`node-gyp` build steps triggered by lifecycle scripts (`install` typically) — compile arbitrary C++ source shipped in the package. Native code runs in the developer's environment.
A dependency's `node_modules` bundled inside its own tarball — every entry point above applies recursively to the inner tree. Missed if the engine only walks the outer tree (packet exploit N1).

graph.description: A direct dependency listed in the consumer's `dependencies` — the obvious surface.
A dependency-of-a-dependency (any depth). event-stream (depth 2) shows why this is not optional.
A dependency listed under `optionalDependencies` — installed on best-effort, but STILL installed by default; every entry point applies.
A dependency listed under `peerDependencies` — installed by npm ≥7 automatically; must be traversed.
A dependency listed under `bundledDependencies`/`bundleDependencies` — the tarball ships its own node_modules copy (see also `bundled-node-modules` above).
A dependency spec pointing at a git URL (`git+https://…`, `git+ssh://…`, `github:owner/repo`). Arbitrary commits — not registry-vetted.
A dependency spec pointing at an HTTP URL — tarball fetched from an arbitrary host.
A local or remote tarball spec (`.tgz`) — no registry provenance.
An `npm:` alias in the version spec (`"foo": "npm:actual-name@1.0.0"`) — attackers use this to make the manifest and the resolved package disagree.
A `link:` protocol spec — creates a symlink to an on-disk directory. The linked source has full entry-point surface.
A `file:` protocol spec — installs from an on-disk path. Any package can be substituted at install time.
